title: Fix %% on elaboration severity tasks
body:
```
Ubuntu 22.04.4 LTS
Verilator 5.035 devel rev v5.034-95-g39c3e79a3
```

It appears that the `%%` escape sequence is broken for elaboration severity tasks.

```systemverilog
// test.sv
module a;
    $info("%%"); // incorrectly displays as "%%"
    initial $info("%%"); // correctly displays as "%"
endmodule
```
```log
$ verilator --binary test.sv 
-Info: test.sv:2:5: %%
                  : ... note: In instance 'a'
    2 |     $info("%%");  
      |     ^~~~~
make: Entering directory
...
- V e r i l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.035 devel rev v5.034-95-g39c3e79a3
- Verilator: Built from 0.022 MB sources in 2 modules, into 0.018 MB in 8 C++ files needing 0.000 MB
- Verilator: Walltime 1.444 s (elab=0.004, cvt=0.013, bld=1.411); cpu 0.009 s on 1 threads; alloced 9.098 MB

$ ./obj_dir/Vtest 
[0] -Info: test.sv:3: a: %
- S i m u l a t i o n   R e p o r t: Verilator 5.035 devel
- Verilator: end at 0s; walltime 0.001 s; speed 0.000 s/s
- Verilator: cpu 0.003 s on 1 threads; alloced 168 MB
```

Thanks!
